_meta.notes: Living document. Update as the brand system evolves. Colors marked 'approximate' are visual estimates — confirm against the official brand book before locking.

identity.name: Chamelo
identity.category: Smart eyewear · tint-adjustable and color-changing lenses
identity.wedge: Direct, on-demand tint control — not auto-reactive, not Transitions. Tap or slide. The lens keeps up.
identity.real_product: We don't sell sunglasses. We sell control — over light, over environment, over how you see the world.
identity.mission_short: Chamelo is leading a new era of smart eyewear, leveraging cutting-edge technology to empower people to see the world on their terms.
identity.vision: A future of eyewear that fuses cutting-edge technology with personal style, redefining the industry and empowering individuals to express their unique vision of the world.
identity.essence: Game-changing, performance-enhancing, and mood-boosting sunglasses. Made with next-gen Chamelo® lenses, created to help you see better.

colors._note: Approximate hex codes derived from visual inspection of chamelo.com. Replace with exact brand-book values when confirmed.

typography._note: Chamelo.com runs on Shopify; the live site uses a clean grotesque sans for UI and body. Exact font family not confirmed in scrape — best stand-ins below until brand book confirms.
typography.headline_style: Editorial serif used sparingly for premium headlines. Optical-size aware. Italic for sub-heads and accent phrases.
typography.ui_style: Geometric grotesque. Medium weight for navigation, regular for body, semibold for stats.

logo.typography: Custom or modified geometric sans (to be confirmed against brand book)
treatments: White wordmark on black background (footer, dark sections)
logo.clear_space: TBD — match the wordmark height as minimum padding on all sides until brand book confirms

pillars.name: Confident and Knowledgeable
pillars.summary: Authoritative, bold, approachable. Make complex tech easy to understand. Skip hedging. Make claims specific.
do: Use exact specs ('0.1 seconds', '63% to 17% VLT')

pillars.name: Fun and Young-at-Heart
pillars.summary: Lively, youthful energy. Light-hearted, playful, vibrant. Pattern-interrupt copy wins on social.
do: Single exclamation point when it lands

pillars.name: Passionate and Personal
pillars.summary: Warm, engaging, personal. Connect, don't sell. Use real customer names.

pillars.name: Keep It Real
pillars.summary: Authentic, sincere, straightforward. Boldness with integrity. Same energy as Stephon Marbury.
dont: Script ambassadors out of their natural voice

triples: Four colors. One tap. Instant calm.
Built for miles, not couch time.
benefit_first: Adjust lens tint in 0.1 seconds (not 'Eclipse™ electrochromic lens technology')
From bright to blackout in 0.1s (not '63% to 17% VLT range')
problem_solution: Going in and out of tunnels used to mean swapping glasses constantly. Now I just slide and go.
From city chaos to instant calm in 0.1 seconds.
one_frame_many_uses: One frame for every version of you.

banned: Take it to the next level

in_doubt_checklist: Would Marbury say this out loud?
Does this lead with the benefit or the feature? (Choose benefit.)
Could a competitor have written this same line? (If yes, rewrite.)
Is there a banned word in it?

hero: The lens that keeps up with you.
Vision that adapts to your energy.

music_shield: Turn up the pace, not the volume.
Built for miles, not couch time.
aura_calm: From city chaos to instant calm.
aura_audio: Four colors. Premium audio. One tap.
Tap for color. Swipe for sound.
Hear the world. Change the view.
knicks: Built in Blue. Tuned for Orange.
general: From shadow to shine in a tap.
Lenses that adapt before you can blink.

product_universe.name: Aura
product_universe.hero_feature: Four-color switch (the hook — frame it as mood, identity, instant transformation)
product_universe.positioning: Mood-changing eyewear. The lens palette choice is the mood choice.

product_universe.name: Music Shield Gen 2
product_universe.hero_feature: Tint slider (always the hero) — audio is a supporting benefit, not the lead
product_universe.positioning: Tint-adjustable sport sunglasses with open-ear audio

product_universe.name: Knicks Edition
product_universe.positioning: Built in Blue. Tuned for Orange.

ambassadors.name: Stephon Marbury
ambassadors.role: Chief Brand Officer · Two-time NBA All-Star
ambassadors.works: Blind reactions, genuine surprise, off-the-cuff testimonials, basketball references
ambassadors.doesnt_work: Scripted product walkthroughs, generic hype-man energy

ambassadors.name: Alycia Baumgardner
ambassadors.works: Training environments, championship credibility, women's empowerment without preaching
ambassadors.doesnt_work: Forcing a fashion-first frame on a fighter

rules: Soul ID training required for any AI-generated human likeness — no generic AI faces
Figma finishing required for any AI-generated visual — no raw Higgsfield/kie.ai outputs
AI-generated voices are NOT used in paid ads — real ambassadors, real VO, real customers only
Every AI-generated ad carries Meta's AI Content Label per policy
Bar is 'indistinguishable from production photography', not 'good enough for AI'
